source.title: Collateral pathways from the ventromedial hypothalamus mediate defensive behaviors.

confidence_rationale: Definitive circuit-tracing paper showing VMHdm SF1+ neurons send collateral projections that produce DIFFERENT defensive behaviours via DIFFERENT downstream targets: VMHdm -> dlPAG drives immobility/freezing; VMHdm -> AHN drives avoidance. This is the canonical demonstration that a single upstream broadcast splits at downstream nodes into distinct behavioural commits, exactly the architecture SD-032a SalienceCoordinator should arbitrate. Confidence high because the dissection is causal (optogenetic terminal stimulation) and uses fluorescent retrograde tracers to identify true collateral projections.
failure_signatures: Single VMHdm cell sends collaterals to multiple downstream targets, each driving a distinct defensive sub-behaviour -- broadcast architecture from single source.
Different terminal stimulation produces different behaviour from the SAME upstream population -- arbitration happens downstream, not at the source.

mapping.source_claim_statement: VMHdm SF1+ neurons (predator-responsive) send collateral projections to anterior hypothalamic nucleus (AHN), dorsolateral PAG (dlPAG), and other targets. Optogenetic activation of VMHdm -> dlPAG terminals drives immobility; VMHdm -> AHN terminals drive avoidance. Distinct defensive sub-behaviours are produced by terminal stimulation of the SAME upstream cells.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the canonical broadcast-with-downstream-arbitration architecture: one source (VMHdm), three+ outputs (dlPAG, AHN, others), each driving a different behaviour. For V3, this is direct evidence that z_harm-driven defensive behaviours emerge from downstream arbitration, not from segregated upstream channels. SD-032a SalienceCoordinator's role is to be the arbiter at the downstream nodes, weighting which target's behaviour wins. MECH-279's PAG GABAergic gate is one such downstream arbiter (the freeze-vs-no-freeze gate at dlPAG).
mapping.mapping_caveat: VMHdm is innate-threat-specific (predator); whether other harm sources (pain, malaise, social threat) also broadcast through this same architecture or use parallel architectures is a separate question. The override question (what suppresses VMHdm or its downstream gates under hunger) is not addressed here.
mapping.source_context: Optogenetic terminal stimulation, retrograde tracing with cholera toxin B and AAV, behavioural quantification in mice exposed to social threat and innate predator cues.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron paper, definitive circuit dissection by the Lin lab. Mapping fidelity high for broadcast-with-downstream-arbitration architecture.
